# Claude Code prompt — [5A] Quote → Scope items redesign — discipline-as-grouping + per-card add + DnD + read-only labels + simple-mode rename

*Paste below the line. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-28.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow it.

## Background

The Quote → Edit → **Scope items** sub-tab currently presents scope items in a flat table grouped by discipline header (ASB (4), Civ (1), CIV (2), …) with columns Label / Description / Qty / Unit / Notes / Source. Several UX issues:

1. The top-level **"+ Add item"** button creates a flat scope item with no discipline binding. Owner wants it to instead create a **new discipline grouping** (a parent row that scope items hang off).

2. There's no per-discipline "+ Add row" — to add a scope item within a specific discipline, the user has no obvious affordance.

3. The **Label column** (ASB1.1, Civ1.1, etc. — these are wbsCodes) is currently editable. It shouldn't be — the wbsCode is a key identifier used to trace the item back to the Scope of Works tab where the calculation came from. Editing it would break traceback.

4. The **Source column** duplicates the Label column (both show the same wbsCode in the screenshots). The Source column is redundant — once Label is read-only it IS the source identifier. Drop the Source column entirely.

5. Scope items should be **drag-and-droppable between disciplines**. If an estimator decides Civ1.1 is actually a Demolition item, they should be able to drag it into the DEM grouping. **Critical:** the wbsCode (`Civ1.1`) does NOT change on drag — it stays `Civ1.1` because the wbsCode is the back-reference to the Scope of Works calculation. The drag only updates the **displayed grouping** on the quote.

6. On the **Simple mode** view (when `Quote detail level` is set to "Simple — no scope table on PDF"), the Cost summary table shows rolled-up discipline rows (Demolition / Civil works / Asbestos removal / Other / Concrete cutting). Owner wants these **descriptions to be renameable** in Simple mode — so if the estimator wants "Demolition" to read "Internal strip-out and demolition" on the client PDF, they can change it without affecting the underlying discipline code.

## Phase 0

- `git fetch origin`; branch off `main`: `feat/quote-scope-items-redesign`.
- Conflict-check the Quote edit components and the `QuoteScopeItem` model in Prisma.

## Schema

`QuoteScopeItem` (or whatever the underlying model is called — look in `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` for the model that backs the quote's scope-items table) already has `discipline` (or equivalent grouping field) and `wbsCode`. Two additions:

1. **`quoteDiscipline String?`** — the displayed grouping on the quote. Independent of `wbsCode`. Initially null/empty; falls back to deriving from wbsCode prefix when null. DnD between disciplines sets this field; wbsCode stays stable. If `quoteDiscipline` is set, item displays under that grouping on Quote → Scope items; if null, displays under the discipline derived from wbsCode.

2. **`displayDescription String?`** on the Cost Summary line model (`QuoteCostLine` or equivalent) — used only in **Simple mode** to override the default discipline-name rollup. If null, fall back to the standard discipline name. If set, that string is what shows on the client PDF Cost summary table.

Migration: auto-generated full-timestamp (`pnpm --filter @project-ops/api prisma migrate dev --name quote_scope_redesign`). Pure additive (two nullable string fields).

## API

Extend the relevant endpoints:

- `PATCH /api/v1/tenders/:tenderId/quotes/:quoteId/scope-items/:itemId` — accept new `quoteDiscipline` field. When updated, no schema-side validation needed (free-form string); the UI restricts choices to the active disciplines on the quote.

- `PATCH` on the Cost line model — accept new `displayDescription` field. Same flexibility.

## UI — Detailed mode (Scope items sub-tab)

Replace the current flat table with a **grouped table**, one section per discipline:

```
Demolition (3)
[ + Add row ]
  ┌──────────┬────────────────────────────────────┬─────┬──────┬───────┐
  │ DEM1.1   │ Internal strip-out — remove …       │ 750 │ m²   │ …      │  ⋮ drag-handle
  │ DEM1.2   │ Structural demolition — load-…      │ 50  │ m³   │ …      │  ⋮
  │ Civ1.5   │ (dragged from Civ) Service …        │ 12  │ Lm   │ …      │  ⋮ ← still wbsCode = Civ1.5
  └──────────┴────────────────────────────────────┴─────┴──────┴───────┘

Civil Works (2)
[ + Add row ]
  ┌──────────┬────────────────────────────────────┬─────┬──────┬───────┐
  │ Civ1.1   │ Trench excavation …                 │ …   │ …    │ …      │  ⋮
  │ Civ1.2   │ Service reinstatement …             │ …   │ …    │ …      │  ⋮
  └──────────┴────────────────────────────────────┴─────┴──────┴───────┘

Asbestos Removal (4) …
Other (0) …

[ + Add discipline ]   ← was "+ Add item"
```

Behaviour:

- **Top-level "+ Add discipline"** (replaces today's "+ Add item"): opens a small popup similar to PR #5's discipline picker. User picks Demolition / Civil Works / Asbestos Removal / Other. On confirm, an empty grouping appears (no items yet) so the user can add items to it.

- **Per-discipline "+ Add row"**: adds a blank scope item to that discipline. wbsCode is auto-generated using that discipline's prefix and next sequential number (e.g. new item in Civil Works = Civ1.4 if Civ1.1, Civ1.2, Civ1.3 already exist). `quoteDiscipline` is set to that discipline.

- **Label column** (wbsCode): rendered as plain text, NOT an input. Visually distinguishable as read-only. The label is hidden from the quote PDF entirely (it's UI-only for traceback).

- **Source column**: removed. Drop the column header and the cell.

- **Description, Qty, Unit, Notes columns**: stay editable as today.

- **Drag handle (⋮ or grip icon)** at the start of each row. Items can be dragged within their current discipline (reorder) OR onto another discipline section header to move between disciplines. On drop into a new discipline section, set the item's `quoteDiscipline` to that section's discipline code via PATCH. wbsCode is unchanged.

Use a lightweight DnD library — likely `@dnd-kit/core` + `@dnd-kit/sortable` if not already in the project, or whatever DnD library the app uses elsewhere (search the app for existing DnD usage first).

## UI — Simple mode (Cost summary table editable descriptions)

When `Quote detail level === "Simple"`, the Cost summary table on the Quote view shows rolled-up disciplines:

```
SCOPE     DESCRIPTION              AMOUNT (EX GST)
DEM       Demolition                       $52,441.43
CIV       Civil works                      $20,010.00
ASB       Asbestos removal                 $28,710.00
Other     Other                            $58,000.00
Cutting   Concrete cutting                  $2,143.67
```

In edit mode, the **DESCRIPTION** column for each row becomes an editable text input. Default value = the discipline's standard name. On change, persist to `displayDescription` on the Cost line via PATCH. The SCOPE column (DEM, CIV, ASB, Other, Cutting) is read-only — it's the discipline code, never edited.

On client PDF render, if `displayDescription` is set, use it; otherwise fall back to the standard discipline name.

## Quote PDF render — exclude Label column

Wherever the quote PDF Scope-of-works table is rendered (search for the Scope items table render in `apps/api/src/modules/client-quotes/quote-pdf.*` or the template), confirm the **Label / wbsCode is NOT printed** on the client PDF. The Source column was already PDF-only metadata; with Source removed and Label being UI-only, neither should appear on the rendered PDF. Items appear on the PDF with Description / Qty / Unit / Notes only, grouped by `quoteDiscipline` (or by derived discipline if `quoteDiscipline` is null).

## Tests

- Schema: migration applies; `quoteDiscipline` and `displayDescription` are nullable strings.
- API: PATCH accepts new fields, persists, returns updated row.
- UI Detailed mode: "+ Add discipline" opens picker, "+ Add row" appends item to correct group with correct wbsCode, drag handle moves items between groups, wbsCode unchanged on drag, Label rendered as plain text (no input).
- UI Simple mode: description cells are editable in edit mode, persist on save, PDF uses overridden description if set.
- PDF render: Label column not present on PDF; items grouped by quoteDiscipline.
- §6 pre-PR CI checklist green.

## PR

- Title: `[5A] Quote → Scope items redesign — discipline grouping + per-card add + DnD + simple-mode rename`
- Body: walk through the six changes. Emphasise that wbsCode is intentionally stable on DnD (traceback to Scope of Works tab — Quote pulls data, doesn't push).
- Reviewer `GH-Mantova`.
- **Do NOT auto-merge.** Owner verifies live in both Detailed and Simple modes, including a DnD between disciplines and a Simple-mode description override.

## Scope

Only the Quote → Edit Scope items sub-tab redesign + Simple mode cost summary rename. Do not change the underlying Scope of Works calculation tab, do not touch the Provisional Sums / Cost Options / Assumptions / Exclusions / T&C tabs (those are separate, already touched by PR-8).

## Notes for review

- The new `quoteDiscipline` is intentionally a free-form string (not a strict enum) so future disciplines can be added without schema changes. The picker UI restricts to the current four (DEM / CIV / ASB / Other) but the data layer is flexible.
- If a user moves Civ1.5 into Demolition then later the source Civ1.5 row is deleted from Scope of Works, the quote scope item should stay (with its wbsCode still reading `Civ1.5` as a dangling back-ref). This is acceptable — the quote is a snapshot. Owner can manually delete the dangling row if needed.
